name: Oratorio de Valdecanales along the Giribaile reservoir bank - circular from Rus
name_original: Oratorio de Valdecanales por la orilla del embalse de Giribaile - circular desde Rus
region_intro: Touring cycling around Rus is characterized by varied terrain, featuring areas around reservoirs and historical landmarks. The landscape includes rolling hills with notable ascents, providing diverse routes for cyclists. These routes often traverse open countryside, offering views of the surrounding region. The area's topography supports a range of cycling experiences, from moderate paths to more challenging climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Rus The most popular touring cycling route is Oratorio de Valdecanales along the Giribaile reservoir bank - circular from Rus, a 20.1 miles (32.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows the bank of the Giribaile reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Small Rest Area – Bike Path Footbridge loop from Rus, a moderate 21.0 miles (33.9 km) path. This route includes sections along a bike path and features a footbridge. Local touring cyclists also love the Church of San Pedro – Castillo de Sabiote loop from Baeza, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) trail leading through historical landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around Rus is defined by routes around reservoirs, rolling hills, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Rus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Rus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Rus?
A: There are over 75 touring cycling routes around Rus, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes often feature varied terrain, including areas around reservoirs and historical landmarks, with rolling hills and notable ascents.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or families in Rus?
A: Yes, Rus offers a few easier touring cycling routes. While many routes involve rolling hills and ascents, there are at least 3 routes categorized as easy. For a moderate option that includes a bike path and a footbridge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Rest Area – Bike Path Footbridge loop from Rus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Rus?
A: Touring cycling routes in Rus are characterized by varied terrain. You'll find paths around reservoirs, rolling hills with notable ascents, and routes that traverse open countryside. Many routes also lead through areas rich in historical landmarks.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore along the touring cycling routes in Rus?
A: The region around Rus is rich in history. You can cycle past significant sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Chapel of the Savior&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7245052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baeza Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pablo Church in Úbeda&lt;/a&gt;. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20125366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro – Castillo de Sabiote loop from Baeza&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight historical areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Rus?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Rus are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994112663/oratorio-de-valdecanales-along-the-giribaile-reservoir-bank-circular-from-rus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oratorio de Valdecanales along the Giribaile reservoir bank - circular from Rus&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop around the reservoir.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Rus?
A: The touring cycling routes in Rus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views around reservoirs, and the opportunity to explore historical landmarks.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region features several routes that follow the banks of reservoirs, providing picturesque views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994112663/oratorio-de-valdecanales-along-the-giribaile-reservoir-bank-circular-from-rus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oratorio de Valdecanales along the Giribaile reservoir bank - circular from Rus&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering extensive views of the Giribaile reservoir.

Q: Can I find routes with significant elevation changes for a more challenging ride?
A: Yes, Rus is known for its rolling hills and notable ascents, making it suitable for cyclists looking for a challenge. Q: What castles or historical sites can I visit near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, the area around Rus is home to several castles and historical sites. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5302875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Castle&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7895201" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo De Canena&lt;/a&gt;, and historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6733648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Úbeda&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6735333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arab Tower&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Rus?
A: Yes, several routes offer opportunities to enjoy panoramic views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10320491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Rest Area – Plaza de Toros Viewpoint loop from Rus&lt;/a&gt; includes a viewpoint that provides expansive vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: How long do touring cycling routes in Rus typically take to complete?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their length and difficulty. For example, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Rest Area – Bike Path Footbridge loop from Rus&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 21 miles (33.9 km), typically takes around 2 hours 15 minutes. More challenging routes can take 3 hours or more.
tips:
The reservoir is also known as Mar de La Loma and is located in the Guadalquivir Basin where the famous Ariza Bridge from the 16th century is submerged inside.

Currently there is a tourist jetty, bathing and fishing areas.

Hermitage or cave monastery of Visigothic origin located in the municipality of Rus in the province of Jaén.
Its construction dates between the sixth and seventh centuries, and it is the only Visigothic hypogeum in southern Spain.
